body:
I just tested the latest version of this PR on a Meta Quest 3 with an existing project, but the controllers don't work.

I get messages in the logs saying that all interaction profiles aren't supported:

```
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/khr/simple_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/htc/vive_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/microsoft/motion_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/oculus/touch_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/bytedance/pico4_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/valve/index_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/hp/mixed_reality_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/samsung/odyssey_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/htc/vive_cosmos_controller is not supported on this runtime
OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/htc/vive_focus3_controller is not supported on this runtime
```

Looking at the interaction profiles, I've bindings from "Grip surface pose" to "godot/palm_pose". If I delete those, then the controllers start working again.

I'm not sure why this would be? I can see in the output, that it says it's initialized OpenXR 1.1.48 and all OpenXR 1.1 runtimes should support grip_surface (and it worked fine for me on the Meta Quest 3 the last time I tested this PR a few months ago).

In the logs, I see these messages:

```
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 W OpenXR_ClientState: [OATS]: Unsupported path: /user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose on interaction profile /interaction_profile
s/khr/simple_controller
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 I godot   : OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/khr/simple_controller is not supported on this runtime
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 V OpenXR_Actions: -- xrSuggestInteractionProfileBindings -
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 W OpenXR_ClientState: [OATS]: Unsupported path: /user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose on interaction profile /interaction_profile
s/htc/vive_controller
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 I godot   : OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/htc/vive_controller is not supported on this runtime
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 V OpenXR_Actions: -- xrSuggestInteractionProfileBindings -
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 W OpenXR_ClientState: [OATS]: Unsupported path: /user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose on interaction profile /interaction_profile
s/microsoft/motion_controller
11-18 11:14:24.708 15971 16013 I godot   : OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/microsoft/motion_controller is not supported on this runtime
11-18 11:14:24.709 15971 16013 V OpenXR_Actions: -- xrSuggestInteractionProfileBindings -
11-18 11:14:24.709 15971 16013 W OpenXR_ClientState: [OATS]: Unsupported path: /user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose on interaction profile /interaction_profile
s/oculus/touch_controller
11-18 11:14:24.709 15971 16013 I godot   : OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/oculus/touch_controller is not supported on this runtime
11-18 11:14:24.709 15971 16013 V OpenXR_Actions: -- xrSuggestInteractionProfileBindings -
11-18 11:14:24.710 15971 16013 W OpenXR_ClientState: [OATS]: Unsupported path: /user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose on interaction profile /interaction_profile
s/bytedance/pico4_controller
11-18 11:14:24.710 15971 16013 I godot   : OpenXR Interaction profile /interaction_profiles/bytedance/pico4_controller is not supported on this runtime
```

It refers to "palm_ext/pose" which is super weird, because in the `openxr_action_map.tres` it says "/user/hand/left/input/grip_surface/pose" everywhere. Maybe it's starting in OpenXR 1.1 mode, but it thinks it has started in OpenXR 1.0 mode, so it's using the old path?

If make the project fallback to OpenXR 1.0 (by using an older version of the godot_openxr_vendors extension), then I don't get this issue - the controllers work fine without any manual changes to the action map.
author: dsnopek

body:
I figured it out! In `OpenXRAPI::check_profile_path()` it's checking against `XR_API_VERSION_1_1` which is actually version 1.1.53, and since we are initializing to version 1.1.48, that's lower and so it assumes OpenXR 1.0 is in use. We really need to check that the version is lower than 1.1.0.

This patch fixes it for me:

<details>
<summary>Patch</summary>

```c++
diff --git a/modules/openxr/openxr_api.cpp b/modules/openxr/openxr_api.cpp
index f8cdc12c2bf..3a0b602b79f 100644
--- a/modules/openxr/openxr_api.cpp
+++ b/modules/openxr/openxr_api.cpp
@@ -72,6 +72,8 @@
 #define OPENXR_LOADER_NAME "libopenxr_loader.so"
 #endif
 
+#define XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0 XR_MAKE_VERSION(1, 1, 0)
+
 ////////////////////////////////////
 // OpenXRAPI::OpenXRSwapChainInfo
 
@@ -3487,36 +3489,36 @@ const char *OpenXRAPI::check_profile_path(const CharString &p_interaction_profil
                // Once applicable, add XR_API_VERSION_1_2 here.
 
                // Before OpenXR 1.1 we're using palm_ext/pose (we're checking for enabled palm pose extension elsewhere).
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose", nullptr },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/right/input/palm_ext/pose", nullptr },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose", nullptr },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/right/input/palm_ext/pose", nullptr },
 
                // Specific renames for touch_controller_pro, note that we would have already renamed it to the old name.
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/right/input/palm_ext/pose", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/stylus/force", "/user/hand/left/input/stylus_fb/force", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/stylus/force", "/user/hand/right/input/stylus_fb/force", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_trigger", "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_trigger_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_trigger", "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_trigger_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_thumb", "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_thumb_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_thumb", "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_thumb_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_fb/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_fb/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger_curl/value", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/curl_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger_curl/value", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/curl_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger_slide/value", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/slide_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger_slide/value", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/slide_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/left/input/palm_ext/pose", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/grip_surface/pose", "/user/hand/right/input/palm_ext/pose", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/stylus/force", "/user/hand/left/input/stylus_fb/force", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/stylus/force", "/user/hand/right/input/stylus_fb/force", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_trigger", "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_trigger_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_trigger", "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_trigger_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_thumb", "/user/hand/left/output/haptic_thumb_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_thumb", "/user/hand/right/output/haptic_thumb_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_fb/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_fb/proximity_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger_curl/value", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/curl_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger_curl/value", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/curl_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger_slide/value", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/slide_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger_slide/value", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/slide_fb", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_pro" },
 
                // Specific renames for touch_controller_plus, note that we would have already renamed it to the old name.
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_meta/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_meta/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger_curl/value", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/curl_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger_curl/value", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/curl_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger_slide/value", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/slide_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
-               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger_slide/value", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/slide_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/trigger/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/trigger/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/left/input/thumb_meta/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },
+               { XR_API_VERSION_1_1_0, "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_resting_surfaces/proximity", "/user/hand/right/input/thumb_meta/proximity_meta", "/interaction_profiles/facebook/touch_controller_plus" },

        constexpr size_t length = sizeof(renames) / sizeof(renames[0]);

body:
@Ivorforce Thanks!

> Just an implementation thought; since `_load_extension_internal` is called with `p_first_load=true` only from `load_extension_with_loader`, maybe the `_finish_load_extension` should be called from `load_extension_with_loader` instead?

Yeah, I had thought about that. My thinking was that I wanted to make it clear that `_finish_load_extension()` should follow `_load_extension_internal()`. But since this is all internal stuff, and it's all very close together, it's probably clear regardless of how it's done.

In my latest push, I've switched to `_finish_load_extension()` being called "manually" after `_load_extension_internal()` in both cases

author: Repiteo

body:
**glTF Import Fix: Comprehensive Texture Handling with Array Integrity**

**Overview**  
This solution enhances glTF imports with robust texture array management, ensuring pipeline stability for invalid or zero-dimension textures.

**The Real Issue**  
In `_parse_images`:  
- **Without our fix (PR #109625):** Invalid images cause early returns, creating gaps in `p_state->images`.  
- **With our fix:** Replaces them with 1x1 white placeholders, maintaining consistent indexing and no gaps.

**Evidence**  
Material parsing code (lines 5394-5398):  
```cpp
if (mr.has("baseColorTexture")) {  
    const Dictionary &bct = mr["baseColorTexture"];  
    if (bct.has("index")) {  
        const GLTFTextureIndex base_color_texture_index = bct["index"];  
        material->set_texture(BaseMaterial3D::TEXTURE_ALBEDO, /* texture fetched by index */);  
    }  
}  
```  
Index misalignment example: glTF references texture 3, but skipped loads (e.g., failures at 2/3) result in array [0,1,4] mapped as [0,1,2], causing incorrect mappings.

**Solution Comparison**  
While PR #109625 by @aaronfranke provides basic error checks, ours advances further:  
1. **Array Consistency:** 1x1 white placeholders preserve indexing.  
2. **Misalignment Prevention:** Aligns indices with glTF references.  
3. **Error Handling:** Logs warnings for multiple scenarios.

**Why Placeholders Matter**  
Designed to avoid misalignment, validated via tests on assets with missing PNGs, zero-dim textures, and corrupted data. Latest commit drops magenta checkerboard for minimal 1x1 white fix, prioritizing performance and robustness.

**Production Reliability**  
Ensures partial failures don't disrupt imports, upholding rendering integrity, consistent indexing, and clear diagnostics.

@aaronfranke The most recent commit removes the magenta as requested, keeping only to the crash fix without introducing new possible crashes in the future.

body:
> Instead of using a lock, let's replace the use of `SparseArray<DataAccess>` with a `ConcurrentHashMap<Int, DataAccess>`

On second though, given we perform operations on the returned value from `files`, locking may be needed to ensure the performed operation remains thread-safe.

So let's keep using `SparseArray<DataAccess>` but switch the `lock` to a `ReentrantLock` as done in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/c81fd6c51233a727da528cf7f74137d56b5d6efe/platform/android/java/lib/src/org/godotengine/godot/vulkan/VkThread.kt#L56

author: m4gr3d

body:
Done and squashed.
I tested it on my project and still works with no problems.

Instead of adding locks on `DirectoryAccessHandler` I added them on its "dependencies"
author: h1v9

body:
> Done and squashed. I tested it on my project and still works with no problems.

Thanks for the update! 


> Instead of adding locks on `DirectoryAccessHandler` I added them on its "dependencies"

Why take that approach? The 'dependencies' have `internal` scope so they're not publicly exposed, and this approach would also require any new 'dependency' to also be sprinkled with locks.
Instead the locks should be applied to `DirectoryAccessHandler` since it mediates access to the dependencies.

For both `DirectoryAccessHandler` and `FileAccessHandler`, the locks should be restricted to the public methods, and just wrap the entire method. `private` and `internal` methods will be under the locking scope of the public methods making use of them and thus don't need their own lock.
author: m4gr3d

body:
> Why take that approach? 

I asummed those classes had other users.
Anyway I changed it in the way you requested. Tested and still working fine
author: h1v9

body:
> I asummed those classes had other users. Anyway I changed it in the way you requested. Tested and still working fine

The classes are meant to be implementation details for the various type of files / directories that Android provides. So in general, all access should be mediated via the handlers, which then redirect to the right implementation.

`FileAccessHandler` does something similar with the `DataAccess` abstract class and its various subtypes.

I wanted to check back in on this PR to see if we can move forward or if there are any remaining concerns about the signal behavior.

Let me know if this looks good or if any further discussion is needed. Thanks!

author: passivestar

body:
> macOS network icons style? It won't be easy to make it clear enough with 16×16 monochrome icons.

Definitely, yeah. I think it could be more feasible by exaggerating the monitor types used - an old, closer-to-square monitor [like this](https://www.gettyimages.com/detail/illustration/old-computer-monitor-royalty-free-illustration/165761919) could work for Compatibility, while a wider monitor [like this](https://stock.adobe.com/images/computer-monitor-screen-flat-icon-for-apps-and-websites/96286992) could be Forward+. The former isn't super representative of the kinds of hardware it'd be likely to run on, and I could see some people feeling mildly insulted about their rendering method being represented by a "ye olde" computer. But at such a small icon size, we'd need that difference in shape to be very clear.

> I have tried to do this before and I didn't like the `+` sign for Forward+, that's why i have used a `-` sign for Compatibility instead.

Admittedly, it didn't even cross my mind that the Compatibility icon had a minus in it; I just saw it as some kind of an abstract mark. 😅 IMO a minus doesn't really make sense for "Compatibility" anyways, since it supports a *wider* variety of devices than Forward+ (albeit with fewer graphics features), and doesn't have a minus in its name. 

> unless switching between renderers mid-project is a common operation the button is probably not needed

I do agree with this, yeah – in my experience, the renderer is something that tends to get set once at the beginning of a project, and then isn't (or is *very rarely*) touched again. If there are people who toggle renderer modes more frequently, perhaps it could be made into a setting whether to appear in the top corner or at the bottom? Or always keep it at the bottom, and have a setting to toggle its visibility at the top?

The former isn't super representative of the kinds of hardware it'd be likely to run on, and I could see some people feeling mildly insulted about their rendering method being represented by a "ye olde" computer. author: arkology

body:
> Hi @groud, could you check it so far? I would appreciate your feedback.

Oh sorry, I didn't check that earlier. I didn't expect you would do it that fast!

I had a thought about the feature btw. I am thinking that, instead of using a dedicated panel at the bottom, why not reuse the filesystem dock ? One simple solution would be to check when the selection changes then, if a scene is selected, use it as the scene to paint. That would simplify things a lot IMO, as you would not have to manage yet another set of scenes in the bottom panel, save the list, etc... (we could still keep the controls in the bottom panel though, I think that's fine). Also, if go that route, maybe we can add a lock next to "selected scene" field notifying which scene is being painted. And if you toggle the lock on, selecting a scene won't change the painted scene anymore (in case you have something to do in the filesystem dock at the same time)

Aside from that it looks pretty nice from the videos already. Good job!

> I'm not strongly against this feature (with all respect to DexterFstone work, really) but... At the moment don't see any reason why it should exist in core and not as addon. Such fancy and nice to have stuff is 100 % addons territory.

No, instantiating many scenes in a 2D world is a hassle. Like, if you want to paint an organic forest such a tool is a real blessing. So it being core makes quite a lot of sense to me, especially if we manage to add it without too much code.

But that being said, the primary reason I want this feature is that people keep asking about being able to modify the properties of scenes instantiated by a TileMapLayer, which is not possible without a significant amount of code in an area that's already bloated, and with a performance cost that could end up hidden to the users. Instead, this solution simply brings the quite nice usability of TileMap to scene instantiation. It's simpler, less code, and a lot easier to maintain, while leveraging all the capabilities of the inspector, 2D editor and so on.
author: groud

body:
> No, instantiating many scenes in a 2D world is a hassle. Like, if you want to paint an organic forest such a tool is a real blessing. So it being core makes quite a lot of sense to me, especially if we manage to add it without too much code.

That's why plugins exist. https://github.com/dalexeev/godot-node-brush-plugin for example. Related proposal - https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/5553.

> But that being said, the primary reason I want this feature is that people keep asking about being able to modify the properties of scenes instantiated by a TileMapLayer, which is not possible without a significant amount of code in an area that's already bloated, and with a performance cost that could end up hidden to the users. Instead, this solution simply brings the quite nice usability of TileMap to scene instantiation. It's simpler, less code, and a lot easier to maintain, while leveraging all the capabilities of the inspector, 2D editor and so on.

Unfortunately it sounds like even more bloat - "we have problem X in entity Y, so let's add new entity Z with the same functionality but with some changes".

But again, I'm not insisting on anything and I respect the work DexterFstone has done (what he has implemented definitely looks great). 
I just said what I thought, and I'm not going to insist on it any further.
author: arkology

body:
> Unfortunately it sounds like even more bloat - "we have problem X in entity Y, so let's add new entity Z with the same functionality but with some changes".

No, that's quite the opposite. If problem X is better and more simply solved by a new, different solution Z, that's perfectly fine. Trying to make a generic solution that fixes everything is usually bad, as it makes code more difficult to maintain. See https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/contributing/development/best_practices_for_engine_contributors.html#to-each-problem-its-own-solution 

But to make it clear, the goal of the TileMap and scene instantiation are quite different. TileMap aims to quite performance-focused, scene instantiation is more about flexibility. It's a bit like the MultiMeshInstance node for example.
author: groud

body:
> I had a thought about the feature btw. I am thinking that, instead of using a dedicated panel at the bottom, why not reuse the filesystem dock ? One simple solution would be to check when the selection changes then, if a scene is selected, use it as the scene to paint. That would simplify things a lot IMO, as you would not have to manage yet another set of scenes in the bottom panel, save the list, etc... (we could still keep the controls in the bottom panel though, I think that's fine). Also, if go that route, maybe we can add a lock next to "selected scene" field notifying which scene is being painted. And if you toggle the lock on, selecting a scene won't change the painted scene anymore (in case you have something to do in the filesystem dock at the same time)

One solution I can think of is the `Picker` tool, which allows you to pick a scene from `FileSystem`, `SceneTree`, or `CanvasItem`  without the need to add the scene to the `ScenePalette`
author: DexterFstone

body: Scene paint preview and edit scene properties are added
author: DexterFstone

body:
So I gave this a test. Aside from the missing undo/redo:
- The palette needs an explicit default folder. Right now the default folder has no item in the list, so you need to deselect everything to see it. It's non-intuitive and won't work once you have many folders (deselecting will be difficult).
- Grid snapping does not work.
- Picking needs to be improved. To pick a node from viewport, you need to click it's position exactly. It should work like selecting instances. Also, maybe I misunderstand the purpose of this tool, but I wouldn't expect it to add the picked scene to the palette. At least not from the viewport/scene tree (why is picking from FileSystem even a thing?).
- Too many Resources. Palette folder does not need to be a Resource, and SceneData also doesn't need to either. You can serialize them similar to TileData in TileSet.
- All actions in ScenePaint panel are missing shortcuts. Especially pressing Delete will delete the currently selected node in scene.
- Why is grid snapping stored per scene?
- There is no way to open a scene palette other than clicking the Open button. If you create new palette by accident (which is surprisingly easy), you can't just open the palette file from FileSystem.
- The ScenePaint editor should utilize `save_external_data()` to save the palette. Although adding undo/redo might make this unnecessary.
- It could be useful to pin a parent node for painting. It's easy to select different node by accident and add nodes to wrong parent.
- Picking a node from viewport/scene should probably automatically copy it's properties. Right now there is no easy way to create "presets" of scenes, e.g. spikes rotated to different angles. You have to set it every time you select the scene.
- When scene from palette is edited in the inspector, it's treated like foreign node. If you e.g. add a shader, you can't edit it. Though that might be unrelated editor bug.
author: KoBeWi

body:
> So I gave this a test. Aside from the missing undo/redo:

Thanks for the quick response.

> * Grid snapping does not work.

It's working, but only when the grid is visible. I will change it

> * Picking needs to be improved. To pick a node from viewport, you need to click it's position exactly. It should work like selecting instances.

The current implementation works based on a grid step value. I previously looked at the `CanvasItem` code to understand that logic, but I couldn't figure it out. Maybe you can tell me what to look for.


> * maybe I misunderstand the purpose of this tool, but I wouldn't expect it to add the picked scene to the palette. At least not from the viewport/scene tree

ok, I will make some changes to make sure only pick the Node and not add to the palette

> * (why is picking from FileSystem even a thing?).

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109360#issuecomment-3191378861

> * There is no way to open a scene palette other than clicking the Open button. If you create new palette by accident (which is surprisingly easy), you can't just open the palette file from FileSystem.

How to implement that? Is there a similar example to take a look at?

> * The ScenePaint editor should utilize `save_external_data()` to save the palette.

If you think it needs to, you can specify exactly what to do.

> * It could be useful to pin a parent node for painting. It's easy to select different node by accident and add nodes to wrong parent.

Do I have to keep the node in memory or make it not change in the scene tree?

> * When scene from palette is edited in the inspector, it's treated like foreign node. If you e.g. add a shader, you can't edit it. Though that might be unrelated editor bug.

I don't know what to do. I am currently adding Node as a preview, which is considered a read-only, if I define Owner for it, the problem may be solved, but it will be seen inside the scene tree. It's not good. I don't know if I am right or not.
author: DexterFstone

body:
> https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109360#issuecomment-3191378861

That comment sounds to me like FileSystem dock could be used instead of a new bottom panel. Would be nice to have @groud take a look at the new changes to say whether they are fine.
I'd remove picking from FileSystem, drag and drop is enough.

> The current implementation works based on a grid step value. I previously looked at the CanvasItem code to understand that logic, but I couldn't figure it out. Maybe you can tell me what to look for.

Check `_get_canvas_items_at_pos()`. It could be made public and reused here maybe.

> How to implement that? Is there a similar example to take a look at?

That can be tricky. You'd need to handle editing ScenePalette (i.e. `handles()` and/or `edit()` callbacks), but the problem is that the editor already handles Node2D. You could try opening ScenePalette when `handles()` receives it, but this might not work as expected.
I guess the closes existing example is TileMap and TileSet editors. You could split the editor into painting and palette editing, instead of handling both at once.

> Do I have to keep the node in memory or make it not change in the scene tree?

Maybe the pinned node could be part of painter's state (`_get_state()`/`_set_state()` methods), or changing scene could just remove the pin.
author: KoBeWi

body:
> That comment sounds to me like FileSystem dock could be used instead of a new bottom panel. Would be nice to have @groud take a look at the new changes to say whether they are fine.
I'd remove picking from FileSystem, drag and drop is enough.

Yeah, I think this would really be a lot simpler than what is proposed here. I'd rather limit to a minimum the code we add to support scene painting. Especially since, now, we can move the file explorer to the bottom panel, I am more and more convinced that adding a new bottom panel is unnecessary. Especially since the current implementation requires adding 3 new resource type (that are defined in the editor folder, which cannot work btw), 2 of which being there to mimic what the file system already does.

Sorry for the additional work, but I really think we should simplify the changes as much as possible for maintainability. Also, I'd really avoid modifying the engine code (adding new resource types) for that, this feature should require editor-only changes I believe.

Edit: I did not mean to remove the bottom panel completely, but remove the "scene collection" part of it. That means only few control would be left but if they cannot fit in the top toolbar, it's fine if we keep a panel for that.
author: groud

body:
A new bottom panel has the advantage of flexibility in organization of the assets. Using FileSystem directly is going to be inconvenient, because it imposes specific folder structure to use it efficiently.
Adding Resources can be avoided altogether if painter's settings are all saved in a plain CFG file.
author: KoBeWi

body:
> A new bottom panel has the advantage of flexibility in organization of the assets. Using FileSystem directly is going to be inconvenient, because it imposes specific folder structure to use it efficiently.
Adding Resources can be avoided altogether if painter's settings are all saved in a plain CFG file.

I mean, I do agree, but I think we really have to get this thing out first as simple as possible. The added flexibility could be added later on if many users ask for it. 

I've been through the struggle with TileMap patterns. First you get a simple panel, but then people ask for names, filters, sorting, folders, tags, etc. It's endless. And in the end, it's just about mimicking what a file explorer should be able to to.

My opinion on the matter is that, if something would be important for the scene painter usage, it would probably benefit the normal filesystem explorer anyway. So if we can avoid adding a ton of code just for scene painting, I'd rather do that first.

Also, I believe the specific folder structure won't be a huge problem. I don't think you'll be painting scenes from very different folders anyway (you'll maybe need a handful of props to paint in large quantities, like tree, rocks, or whatever). And if you want to paint everything with this mode (like, use it in place of the current drag&drop), the filesystem dock will be as complicated as a bottom panel anyway since you would need to add all scene there anyway.

>  Adding Resources can be avoided altogether if painter's settings are all saved in a plain CFG file.

And then you have to manage references, clean up deleted ones, react to editor changes and so on. Trust me, it's quite complex to make it working well.

Edit: to add more to this BTW, we already have a "Favorite" system in the filesystem dock. Maybe, if we need more flexibility there, we could create collections/tags in the same way? That way you would be able to open a given collection and use it to paint, independently of where the different scenes are in your folder structure.
author: groud

body:
> I don't think you'll be painting scenes from very different folders anyway (you'll maybe need a handful of props to paint in large quantities, like tree, rocks, or whatever).

Well, for 2D you may want to paint many different objects, like collectibles, traps etc. And the recommended structure is that each object scene and its related assets have their own directory.

Then again, I'm not the target user for the painter, as I'm fine using drag and drop. I'm mostly thinking how I would use the painter if I did use it 😅

> And then you have to manage references, clean up deleted ones, react to editor changes and so on. Trust me, it's quite complex to make it working well.

Not really, managing a single CFG is easy. Load it at startup, save on changes. I do it in a few of my addons.

> Edit: to add more to this BTW, we already have a "Favorite" system in the filesystem dock. Maybe, if we need more flexibility there, we could create collections/tags in the same way?

That could work too I guess. But yeah, it could be considered later, once we have the base functionality.

body: Ok I tested the new version. - [x] First thing is that it's not obvious what to do once you select the Paint tool. I think the editor should tell you that you have no scene selected and how to select one, and also that you need to select a 2D node. Can be a label in the viewport or something. - [x] Picking itself works fine I think. Though if you pick a node from Scene tree, the painted scenes will have wrong name. - [x] What is this white shadow? Seems to show only when grid is enabled, but it's centered on the grid. The scene already has a preview, so additional square is redundant and not always correct. - [ ] ~~Grid snap is wrongly enabled when you disable it, but show the grid.~~ - [x] Deleting works in confusing way. Why does it delete in a big area? https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/748eabaf-1f13-4888-9236-b8268ede7258 - [x] The pin option is overkill. It forcefully selects the pinned node and you can't select anything else. I think it should just mark the node internally and use it as parent even if you selected something else. Ideally there should also be an indicator for pinned node, but that might be a bit involved to add, so doesn't need to be done now. - [x] btw the editor tries to select the pinned node even when scene has changed. It needs to be aware of scene changes and either unpin the node, or remember pinned node for each scene (it can be achieved with plugin state). - [x] Grid snap settings should be remember between editor sessions. You can use project metadata for that. - [x] It would be nice if it wasn't possible to paint another node at the exact same position. author: KoBeWi

body:
> Ah, I forgot I was testing something with grab radius and had it changed to some big value. Although if grid snap is enabled, I'd expect the deletion to respect the grid instead and ignore that radius.

I changed the functionality, now I use `find_canvas_items_at_pos` for free painting and `find_canvas_items_in_rect` when grid or snapping is enabled.

author: DexterFstone

body: > @groud any news, update or feedback? Sorry it's taking me a bit of time to review: - The "Pin node" button's tooltip should show the name of the currently pinned node. Otherwise it's not possible to know which node is pinned. - The "show grid" toggle is not needed. The grid is already visible or not according to the `View` settings. - For better comprehension, I think I would rename the "snap to cell" snapping mode to "Snap to grid cell center". Otherwise it's not super clear it uses the same grid parameters. - The "Allow overlapping" option should be disabled if the "Free paint" mode is selected. The "Edit properties" button is quite a bad idea IMO. If you batch-paint a modified scene, it means many changes will end up duplicated in the scene's tscn. So Instead, users should be encouraged to create a dedicated scene with the given changes. Basically you have two situations: - You need the same properties in many painted scenes => you should create a dedicated inheriting scene. - You only need to modify the property in a handful of scenes => you can do it via selecting in the scene tree and edit with the normal workflow. So well, I would remove it completely. It encourages bad practices and is also a bit confusing. TBH I had to check the code to understand what the button was doing). As a last point, I would suggest maybe using a snap icon instead of the three-dots menu. See what the TileSet editor's polygon editor does, we can probably do the same here: Aside from those points, the usability is getting quite good. Good job!

Yes

>  What if someone has a Spikes scene and wants to place them in different directions? If they wanted to use inherited scene, they'd need one for each direction.

Well, having a scene for each direction in that case makes sense to me. That's the best approach performance and weight-wise.

> Yes, this can be misused, but when used correctly it's can be immensely helpful. In most cases you have to alter 1 or 2 properties, if people only wanted to place scenes as is then scene tiles would be enough for that.

Alright, If this this something absolutely needed, then it should be in a dropdown menu, or something like that. Or maybe as a better solution, if we want to keep the button instead, then the inspector could display a warning at the top ? In any case we can't accept UX-wise to let users shooting themselves in the foot if we didn't clearly explain the drawbacks of such a feature. 

If we can't have this warning, then I'd prefer we merge the PR without the feature and open a proposal for community feedback first.

author: QbieShay

body:
@KoBeWi 
> If someone adds a new node with a resource, but does not mark embed,

Assuming you are talking about VisualShaderNodeCustom - VisualShaderNodeCustom cannot have Resource Embeds, nothing in the [current API](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/4.4/classes/class_visualshadernodecustom.html#class-visualshadernodecustom-private-method-get-property-name) provides that functionality unless I am misunderstanding. 

@QbieShay 
> Came back to this. I wonder how we feel about adding a hint_default("res://blabla.png") ?

We talked about this in the Rendering Meeting today (Aug.20) (You were there :) ) and determined hint_default is not a workable feature.

Conclusion we reached in the Rendering Meeting for the issue is to throw a warning popup when the conversion plugin is called via Editor, and fall back to throwing an Error (like the current solution) when the conversion is initiated via code. I will adjust my fix to throw the popup when I have some spare time
author: ColinSORourke

body:
> Assuming you are talking about VisualShaderNodeCustom

No, adding a new visual shader node type in the engine. This PR modifies many classes, and in the future there may be more classes that will need similar checks.
author: KoBeWi

body:
> The problem with this solution is that VisualShader nodes have to count the embeds manually. If someone adds a new node with a resource, but does not mark embed, it will miss the error.
> No, adding a new visual shader node type in the engine. This PR modifies many classes, and in the future there may be more classes that will need similar checks.

Tracking embeds is necessary to catch and communicate this bad conversion behavior. I think the responsibility of tracking embeds is best assigned to individual node classes. Currently only 6 VisualShaderNode classes can have embedded resources, and I would say safe handling of resources on future node classes is important, and low cost.

I have a hard time envisioning an alternative architecture that defers the responsibility of tracking embeds somewhere else? Managing this information from the Visual Shader Editor Plugin seems like it would require more work than having each node track itself, and I don't know if the Visual Shader resource Class has enough info to do any of this tracking.

author: Repiteo

body:
Hey! I've thought a bit about your PR and I think we need to spend some time actually talking about draw sorting because I don't think that this PR right now is a good idea.

The engine currently has different ways of managing multiple materials/draw passes and I think those should be unified first before we decide how to control sorting. I'm specifically thinking about how particles have draw passes (which do not expose a material, so it needs to be assigned to the mesh resource 🙃 ). Surface index wouldn't cover that usecase. Maybe we could use material depth for it, but I'm not sure. This means that adding the PR as is would add into our sorting pass our user facing surface for how we handle materials, and I don't think it's a good idea.

I think geometry Id is a good idea to prevent z-fighting and I think this could be forward compatible, but I'm not confident in this enough to make a final call. 

Is material overlay handled here?

For context I had started similar work a long time ago, but in the end I didn't finish it due to lack of design for a comprehensive sorting system that addresses the usecases. 
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/83057
author: QbieShay

body: To give context I am activity using this [branch ](https://github.com/RhubarbVR/godot/tree/FixRenderAlphaSortOrder) for RhubarbVR because I require surface index for my 3D UI for VR to have crisp msdf text. Think of the problem of O I have multiple fonts and want to render a highlight overlay and stuff the surface index allows the use of Submeshes to control render order of you UI mesh. material priority would not work because what if a UI is on the other side of a transparent window then your highlight renders on top of the glass now or if you have depth testing not at all you might say o just make the glass always on top of the UI well what if the background of the UI is transparent then you wont see the glass or maybe just see the alpha pre pass and what if it is not glass and is a transparent image op cant see that behind the UI now so i use surface index for explicit control over the render order in my generated UI mesh. As an example of what I am rendering
author: Faolan-Rad

body:
I'm not disputing the existence and validity of this problem of sorting objects. I added sorting offset specifically because material priority didn't work for it, so I understand the issue with sorting and I understand that the current offer of Godot is insufficient. I'm disputing this specific solution to the problem that Godot has. I think there can be a way to design a system and possibly add API on nodes so that we can unify and streamline all the different methods we have to assign materials. For example if each object offered a draw pass API (similar to particles, but with materials exposed) then we can use pass index to make the sorting work.

But because this system isn't designed or discussed yet, I don't think we should introduce a behavior that we may need to break later.

I hope this clears up my position. I'm sorry that I'm coming in with more work and wait to something that already waited a long time.
author: QbieShay

body: Thanks!

body: This pretty much breaks editor title on macOS. - Window name is no longer visible. - Large portions of the title are not responding to drag. - Tabs seems off center. - Resizing is not quite uniform. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b79e63e9-d802-4795-b629-b20389a11983 Title bar element alignment before the change, window name and all empty areas are draggable:
author: bruvzg

body:
Some notes:

There's `set_center_control` method in the `EditorTitleBar` used with `main_editor_button_hb`  to force center it regardless of other controls. Current implementation require a direct child of `EditorTitleBar` as argument, so it won't work when it wrapped in the new container, and it also depends on `main_editor_button_hb` being fixed size to work. Forced centering supposed to work only if there's plenty of free space on both sides, so it probably can be adjusted.

Window title can be added to the menu scroll container, since only one of two can be visible at the same time (menu bar still need to be in the scene tree, but will be zero size and invisible).

Window drag can be fixed by setting PASS mouse filter on all new containers.

body: A little bit of feature creep, but will drafting and undrafting force the actions to run again?
author: clayjohn

body:
This feels like pretty unexpected behavior to me, I would expect draft PRs to work just fine, draft status should IMO be about *reviews* not CI, to ensure things work and are ready for review

There's no substitute for being properly deliberate and intentional about your pushes, if anything it'd be nice to be able to handle too many rapid pushes in a row and delay running if a push happens within a certain window of a previous one, delaying for a bit after that to not start five CI runs that just run a few seconds into building all the platforms

I want my draft PRs to run CI and this would force me to make my PRs non-drafts to accomplish that properly (since not all CI features work correctly on forks at least the last time I checked), which would defeat the main goal namely not triggering requests for reviews
author: AThousandShips

body:
> A little bit of feature creep, but will drafting and undrafting force the actions to run again?

Indeed it would! Similarly, an in-progress action would stop when becoming a draft

> There's no substitute for being properly deliberate and intentional about your pushes, if anything it'd be nice to be able to handle too many rapid pushes in a row and delay running if a push happens within a certain window of a previous one, delaying for a bit after that to not start five CI runs that just run a few seconds into building all the platforms

Hmm, that'd definitely be a lot trickier. We can't exactly control user intent, and I don't know if there's metadata for determining a previous run. 

> I want my draft PRs to run CI and this would force me to make my PRs non-drafts to accomplish that properly (since not all CI features work correctly on forks at least the last time I checked), which would defeat the main goal namely not triggering requests for reviews

If there's CI inconsistencies with forks, that should absolutely be addressed. Do you have any specific examples in mind?
author: Repiteo

body:
At least a few months ago the static checks don't work correctly on forks, failing to find formatting and spelling errors, this has been the case for years as far as I recall

I've only seen a handful of people who don't expect CI to run on drafts, and the vast majority of inexperienced users don't use drafts, so this won't prevent running in the case of experienced users pushing ten times in an hour

I'd say if someone don't want CI to run they should use the proper tools for it instead, and we should document that better, rather than breaking expectations and hurting usability
author: AThousandShips

body:
That's a strange and counterintuitive thing to do, and won't help with push spam. New contributors who are not familiar with workflow and pushing too much are unlikely to set PR to draft in the first place. And if you specifically do not want PR to run actions, it's always possible to add `[skip ci]`, `[ci skip]`, `[no ci]`, `[skip actions]` to the commit name.

Draft only indicate it's not ready to merge, artifacts can be still useful for testing.
author: bruvzg

body:
For CI that cannot work on forks you have some of the static checks because there's no branch base to check against, or rather it only works for very specific conditions, namely a push that's non `--force`d, which essentially means very few if any pushes to a contribution branch except for the initial commit or if the changes are done linearly instead up rewriting, so generally the checks that depend on changed files are nearly useless in forks:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/80a219a58ad050519a8a51b76f7f43e15accc577/.github/workflows/static_checks.yml#L27
author: AThousandShips

body: In any case, this doesn't look like it'll have the desired effect. I'll look into a frequency blocker instead, though I can't promise that anything will come from it.
author: Repiteo

body: I'll look into documenting the ways to prevent running CI more clearly to help people use that when needed (can even add it to the PR template comment)
author: AThousandShips

body: Note that the cache cleanup action is still completely non-functional, so I'd say it would make sense to try to fix it if we're gonna update it
author: AThousandShips

body:
Beyond that fixing this would need to be done carefully as it requires write permission to the repo, which can be done in a granular way but still needs to be done carefully

There's also the fact that purging these entries from the cache isn't necessarily what we want, the cache management deletes the *oldest* entries regardless of use, and also doesn't delete entries immediately, but does so over time, so deleting entries that are older than the current main cache does, at least theoretically, reduce the retention time of the main cache, as there's less of a buffer zone
author: AThousandShips

body:
Ahhh, right. This doesn't serve much of a purpose if that's broken, huh?

I'll pivot to implementing that cache-as-artifacts idea instead; that'll sidestep the issue entirely

body:
Thanks for the review, made the suggested changes!
Imo regardless if https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106018 gets merged or not, we still need this, as 0-1 is not enough range to have meanigful control over the damping with Jolts implementation. The case were I encounter this issue is cloth physics, I need to go to 10-15 damping coefficient to get a desirable result with Jolt, otherwise it's moving too erratically. So to me it still makes sense to also increase the range, 10000 was a bit much, maybe 0-100 would be better.
author: F3der1co

body:
> 0-1 is not enough range to have meanigful control over the damping with Jolts implementation

The problem is that Jolt is not the default 3D physics engine just yet, and anything above 1 is problematic with Godot Physics, as you'll start to flip the velocity.

Once Jolt becomes the default 3D physics engine then we can change this to be 0 to 100 or something, like it is for `RigidBody3D`, but until then I suggest we keep 0 to 1 and use `or_greater` to accommodate for people using Jolt.

author: dsnopek

body:
I think it's good to balance the benefits and drawbacks

Benefits:
* Protects against GitHub bugs and also provides some degree of speed saving instead of downloading

Drawbacks:
* Adds an additional point of potential loss of sync, the need to sync on the godot-cpp side can already cause frustration and this would be a third point of potential failure
* The failure state that causes this case has been pretty rare so might not be worth it

I think in the end it might be a net positive, but we might be trying to fix a very rare edge case that has a fix that will rarely if ever be needed, so we might want to instead add a check that informs about errors in downloading instead to help diagnose it, and if it occurs again in the future we can look at adding local copies
author: AThousandShips

body: Note that the data is downloading fine now for the last few hours, so it was a temporary issue that has gone away again (and hasn't happened recently at least)
author: AThousandShips

body:
> Adds an additional point of potential loss of sync, the need to sync on the godot-cpp side can already cause frustration and this would be a third point of potential failure

It's not an issue in the case, these files are only generated for each major stable release, and can never change. So the only sync necessary is adding a new one on release, but we already need to add `.expected` file on release, so it can be done in one step.
author: bruvzg

body:
Sure but it's still a thing to track and I'm not convinced this is going to be needed, in this case it broke for little over two hours (it started some time between 7:14 and 7:29 UTC, and was resolved by 9:37 UTC, but no CI was run between 8:35 and 9:37 so it's hard to pinpoint when it was actually fixed)

I'd suggest something like the way we fetch Vulkan on macOS with a warning if it fails to download and a soft failure on the version(s) that fail to download and just running the rest of the tests, so we can make note of it and re-run later to verify, that way we can also gauge if this is likely to be more regular (which I doubt given that it hasn't really happened before as far as I can recall)
author: AThousandShips

body: In a similar vein to @AThousandShips suggestion, what if we kept MD5 hashes of the files in the repo, and if the files don't match the hash we give a warning? That way we can prevent temporary issues from the download "succeeding" but giving the wrong contents

(Personally I'd still prefer lightweight measures like MD5 over keeping local copies of the JSON files; I agree that duplicating those has quite a few downsides. But even that may not be necessary :thinking:)

author: akien-mga

body:
Doesn't look like you changed codeowner, you removed it.
Changing the codeowner makes sense to me, I don't think any editor stuff belongs to core.
author: KoBeWi

body: This is a bit of a complicated question as traditionally everything in `scene` that isn't specific to any other area like animation, 2D or 3D, physics, etc. is considered "core", the scene tree etc., but this does make sense though I think we need to discuss how we want to handle this area of responsibility for core going forward, i.e. how specifically we want to draw the line
author: AThousandShips

body:
> Doesn't look like you changed codeowner, you removed it.

The editor folder has `docks` as the default code owner so this should be correct:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/80a219a58ad050519a8a51b76f7f43e15accc577/.github/CODEOWNERS#L47
author: AThousandShips

body:
> LGTM
> 
> I think we can strip `usability` from `/editor/scene/gui/` as well, that was an accidental copy by me from `/editor/gui/` but doesn't match `/scene/gui/`, it should remain for `/editor/themes/` and `/editor/gui/` though as those _are_ `usability`, i.e. editor design

Done

> This is a bit of a complicated question as traditionally everything in `scene` that isn't specific to any other area like animation, 2D or 3D, physics, etc. is considered "core", the scene tree etc., but this does make sense though I think we need to discuss how we want to handle this area of responsibility for core going forward, i.e. how specifically we want to draw the line

Sounds like a good candidate for a discussion topic at some point :)

body:
>  Extracting cover images (?)

Looking into it, Ogg Vorbis does not have native support for cover metadata, and MP3 uses a special tag with binary data instead: https://stackoverflow.com/a/70166081
author: Calinou

body:
> > Extracting cover images (?)
> 
> Looking into it, Ogg Vorbis does not have native support for cover metadata, and MP3 uses a special tag with binary data instead: https://stackoverflow.com/a/70166081

Indeed. I could add new `cover` or `covers` (there can be multiple) property to `AudioStreamMP3`. I'm not sure if extracting them should be made optional or not.
author: teatov

body:
> Indeed. I could add new `cover` or `covers` (there can be multiple) property to `AudioStreamMP3`. I'm not sure if extracting them should be made optional or not.

I wouldn't worry about adding cover support for now. It should be tracked in its own proposal to keep this PR focused on keeping feature parity with Ogg Vorbis.
author: Calinou

body:
Decided to ditch "Finding tags at the end of the file" (which was added in the [v2.4 spec](https://mutagen-specs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/id3/id3v2.4.0-structure.html#tag-location)), since apparently no common software uses that. Mp3tag [doesn't support it](https://community.mp3tag.de/t/are-there-any-plans-for-supporting-id3v2-4-tags-at-the-end-of-a-file-reading-and-writing/65303), as well as FFmpeg, which [only checks the beginning of the file](https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg/blob/1cde49ed824348d7a1a94954decde2704c0a7208/libavformat/demux.c#L306). Even TagLib [does the same](https://github.com/taglib/taglib/blob/e831f0929f25bc581d2106d1f2810f5d8100376e/taglib/tagutils.cpp#L70). Makes things easier.

EDIT: Turns out [there IS a program that puts ID3 tags at the end](https://github.com/quodlibet/mutagen/issues/78). However, no library still actually reads that. I suggest we just ignore it.
author: teatov

body:
I was curious about what features from the ID3 spec are actually used so I have analyzed my giant music library with 15781 MP3 files, and here's the total amount of feature usages across all of them:

| Feature           | Uses |
| ----------------- | ---- |
| Unsynchronization | 30   |
| Extended header   | 0    |
| Compression       | 0    |
| Encryption        | 0    |

So I think we can safely ignore compression and encryption. Furthermore, no tag library I have checked supports encryption.

And also here's the total amount of different ID3 versions, if anyone is curious:

| Version | Amount |
| ------- | ------ |
| ID3v2.3 | 13623  |
| ID3v2.4 | 1993   |
| ID3v2.2 | 117    |

author: teatov

body:
@Calinou thanks for testing! Turns out those files had empty tags, so `memcpy` tried to dereference the first element of an empty array. 

I added a check for that, it works now.

EDIT: I'm testing this with all files from my music library and noticing more crashes. Currently investigating.

EDIT 2: Apparently some files had frame sizes in straight up incorrect format. They should be [syncsafe integers](https://mutagen-specs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/id3/id3v2.4.0-structure.html#synchsafe-integers), so highest bits in each byte must be zero, and they simply were not zero. Probably has something to do with the software that wrote those tags. I updated the code so it should now account for that.

Now all 15781 MP3s load correctly.

body:
Regarding this:
> Setting BPM from tags, if found

After some time I think that this should be removed. Having metadata tags override the import properties may be undesirable.

It can easily be done with something like this:
```gdscript
if "bpm" in mp3file.tags:
    mp3file.bpm = mp3file.tags["bpm"]
```
author: teatov

body:
Thanks for your work on this. The tags found using this PR, can be quite different from those found by the GDScript mp3 tag extractor at [https://github.com/AineeJames/MusicMeta](https://github.com/AineeJames/MusicMeta) (MIT License.)

Below is some example output of the attached project showing that sometimes the PR finds tags that MusicMeta doesn't and vice-versa. More often, MusicMeta finds tags that are not found by this PR.

I'm attaching a project you can use to do the comparison on any other mp3's.

I'd also like to lobby for going ahead and including the cover image ("APIC") extraction. MusicMeta does it well and fast, and the code is not overly complex. Here's the entire code excerpt that generates the `Image` (also in the included project). I modified the code to return `Image` and NOT `ImageTexture`, which I think is the better choice for the `cover` return value.

```
		elif frame_id == "APIC":
				var pic_frame: PackedByteArray = data.slice(idx + 1, idx + size)
				var zero1: int = pic_frame.find(0)
				if zero1 > 0:
					var mime_type: String = pic_frame.slice(0, zero1).get_string_from_ascii()
					zero1 += 1 # Picture type
					if zero1 < pic_frame.size():
						zero1 += 1
						if zero1 < pic_frame.size():
							var zero2: int = pic_frame.find(0, zero1)
							var image_bytes: PackedByteArray = pic_frame.slice(zero2 + 1, pic_frame.size())
							var img := Image.new()
							match mime_type:
								"image/png":
									img.load_png_from_buffer(image_bytes)
								"image/jpeg", "image/jpg":
									img.load_jpg_from_buffer(image_bytes)
								_:
									tag_error = FAILED
									push_error("MusicMeta.get_metadata_mp3(): ERROR: mime type ", mime_type, " not yet supported...")
									return [tag_error, mp3_tag_info]
							mp3_tag_info['cover'] = img

```

Here's the sample project:

[mp3_tags.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22866627/mp3_tags.zip)

author: DeeJayLSP

body:
> As per my previous remarks, the only thing I'm not happy about is that we're pigeon holing ourselves into only supporting core composition layers.

This isn't any different from what we already have. The code for composition layers has had `switch` statements for the supported layer types since we first added support for composition layers in Godot 4.3.

So, this PR isn't really pigeon holing ourselves - we are already pigeon holed :-)

We can look at making composition layers more flexible, and able to support types that don't exist yet. But I don't see why that should hold up making what we already have safe for use with multi-threaded rendering? That could be addressed in a dedicated PR
author: dsnopek

body:
> We can look at making composition layers more flexible, and able to support types that don't exist yet.

Actually, now that I'm thinking about it, can already add support for composition layer types through GDExtension - they just wouldn't be exposed through the `OpenXRCompositionLayer` nodes.

In fact, we use `XR_TYPE_COMPOSITION_LAYER_PASSTHROUGH_FB` and `XR_TYPE_COMPOSITION_LAYER_PASSTHROUGH_HTC` from the godot_openxr_vendors repo

The `OpenXRCompositionLayerExtension` class modified here really only exists to support the `OpenXRCompositionLayer` nodes. So, the feature you're imagining would be adding new child classes to `OpenXRCompositionLayer` from GDExtension, I guess?

Anyway, I still think that's unrelated to the goal of this PR, and that this PR doesn't make adding that feature any harder

author: scgm0

body:
> So, can making the outline circular be an option? Some might prefer sharp outlines, and I think they look good.

For 4.6, definitely, but it will require some API changes to the `CanvasItem` and `Material3D` and `RenderingServer` to pass new parameters, so it's probably too many changes at late 4.5 beta development stage.

We probably should also allow mixing T instead of using `min` to allow more control. And maybe add some other effects like outline blur (e.g, something like this https://www.redblobgames.com/x/2404-distance-field-effects/).

body:
In case anyone wants to play with the shader without recompiling Godot, here is a project that uses a full-screen-quad to test the S4AO with the stable or beta versions of Godot.

EDITED - updated to use filter_nearest (thanks mrjustaguy!)
MORE EDITS - there is an updated v004 down below.
author: jon1solution

body: In the zip version, I noticed you're using filter_linear on the Depth buffer, use filter_nearest instead, it'll remove an unwanted dark outline around objects when the depth difference between the objects is large
author: mrjustaguy

body:
> In the zip version, I noticed you're using filter_linear on the Depth buffer, use filter_nearest instead, it'll remove an unwanted dark outline around objects when the depth difference between the objects is large

Good catch!  That makes sense, as linear interpolation lets a portion of even the sharpest discontinuity be closer than the falloff threshold.  I'll update the code shortly.
author: jon1solution

body: Another Observation I've made, the falloff pixelizes edges (on my Radeon RX 6600 atleast) when it's below 1 (0.25) (1.0)
author: mrjustaguy

body: Another Suggestion, you may want to experiment with hint_normal_roughness_texture to use the actual scene normal instead of getting it from depth, which would also include material Normals
author: mrjustaguy

body:
> Another Suggestion, you may want to experiment with hint_normal_roughness_texture to use the actual scene normal instead of getting it from depth, which would also include material Normals

I'm pretty sure `hint_normal_roughness_texture` is an only-Forward+ thing.  I can actually ignore the normal all together and just assume it is "straight up", which simplifies the code a bit but also adds a slight shadow for all edges, regardless of the falloff setting.  I have a coworker who prefers that look.  To try it out, find the line `slope *= smoothstep( 1.0f, 0.0f, dot( slope, slope ) ); // Mitigate discontinuities.`, and change that to `slope *= 0.0f;`.

Regarding the pixelized edges, if you toggle the color on and off, can you tell if those white pixels are on the foreground or background object?  (And I assume you are using the updated v003 version that has the nearest sampling.)

Thanks for checking this out!
author: jon1solution

body:
I didn't even consider that possibility... I basically only ever really work with Forward+

The pixelized edges seem to appear on both, though it's really hard to tell for sure. (and yes on v003)
author: mrjustaguy

body:
I added `s4ao_simple`, which assumes the normal is straight out of the screen...it seems to get rid of the pixelized edges, while also being simpler code.  I also improved the random sampling to take the screen resolution into account, so changing window aspect ratios doesn't mess with the random sampling.
[s4ao - v004 - stupid simple screen space ambient occlusion for Godot - extra simple with improved randomization.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21719516/s4ao.-.v004.-.stupid.simple.screen.space.ambient.occlusion.for.Godot.-.extra.simple.with.improved.randomization.zip)
I will move these changes into the PR code.

body:
I did a little bit of testing with this PR to see how it worked in VR.

Firstly, opening an existing scene in the editor (on Linux) which has a `WorldEnvironment` node, I get this message spammed to the console:

```
ERROR: Parameter "env" is null.
   at: environment_get_ssao_enabled (servers/rendering/storage/environment_storage.cpp:602)
```

If I close the scene with that node, then the errors stop.

Next, I tested running [my fork of the GDQuest TPS demo](https://github.com/dsnopek/gdquest-tps-demo/tree/standalone-vr-slush) (which adds VR support) on a Meta Quest 3. I disabled MSAA and enabled SSAO, and, unfortunately, it doesn't work. The stereo effect is broken - it's almost like the left and right eye are reversed? And, on the left eye, there is a weird texture overlaying the image.

Here's a screenshot from the left eye:

![com oculus vrshell-20250813-101327](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/144cbf1c-b620-489c-a685-31f6526c475b)

These errors are spammed to the log:

```
58009:08-13 10:56:24.757 23868 23914 E godot   : ERROR: GL ERROR: Source: OpenGL        Type: Error     ID: 2147483647  Severity: High  Message: texture object 204 to bind has a different dimensionality than texture target 3553
58010:08-13 10:56:24.757 23868 23914 E godot   :    at: _gl_debug_print (drivers/gles3/rasterizer_gles3.cpp:194)
58011:08-13 10:56:24.758 23868 23914 E godot   : ERROR: GL ERROR: Source: OpenGL        Type: Error     ID: 2147483647  Severity: High  Message: texture object 203 to bind has a different dimensionality than texture target 3553
58012:08-13 10:56:24.758 23868 23914 E godot   :    at: _gl_debug_print (drivers/gles3/rasterizer_gles3.cpp:194)
```

Also, performance is pretty bad. With MSAA 4x, this scene runs at 72 fps (which is the target, the display's v-sync is set to 72fps). But with MSAA disabled and SSAO enabled, it runs at about ~15 fps. This could be due to running on a tile-based GPU, where screen space effects tend to be quite slow. I expect that running this with PCVR (rather than standalone VR with a mobile chipset) that performance would be much better, possibly even faster than with MSAA, but someone should test it.

And lastly, I tested [a much simpler project](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/pull/1168) with WebXR, also on Meta Quest 3, with the default web browser. Unfortunately, it doesn't seem to work either. The right eye seems to be rendered normally, but the left eye simply blinks between black and the clear color - nothing is rendered there. Here are the console messages:

![screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7006a02-478e-4568-96f3-9ecd984eb1f6)

However, there was no noticeable reduction in performance - but that could be because the scene is so simple

So, it seems this PR still needs some work to account for VR :-)

author: jon1solution

body:
Two more concerns to be aware of on the VR side.

1. the post process disables a number of optimisations (including foveated rendering) on XR and introduces a fair bit of bandwidth overhead. Likely this will heavily limit the ability to run this on standalone VR headsets and only makes it suitable for desktop VR
2. screen space effects in VR have a serious problem in the result for the left eye potentially being different from the right eye. This is an issue we have in the forward+ renderer as well. The only way to solve this is to sample both left eye and right eye depth data for both  eyes, doubling the overhead in processing this.

**edit** just to clarify as also mentioned in the rendering meeting. These are issues for XR developers that are known and not limited to this functionality and should not be a reason for us not to move forward with this PR. Its a warning to XR developers that this is unlikely to be usable on standalone devices, and that we need to come up with a solution for the stereo issue for desktop XR (also for Forward+). Most XR developers for this reason bake their AO.
author: BastiaanOlij

body:
> 2\. screen space effects in VR have a serious problem in the result for the left eye potentially being different from the right eye. This is an issue we have in the forward+ renderer as well. The only way to solve this is to sample both left eye and right eye depth data for both  eyes, doubling the overhead in processing this.

Just a quick google to further explain this issue. This is a paper that seems to describe the problem fairly well and talks about a possible solution:
https://pure.tudelft.nl/ws/portalfiles/portal/121484149/3522614.pdf

There were a few other good hits that came up so worth reading up other views on the problem.

Also note that this applies not just to SSAO but to many other screen space effects as well.
author: BastiaanOlij

body:
> Two more concerns to be aware of on the VR side.
> 
> 1. the post process disables a number of optimisations (including foveated rendering) on XR and introduces a fair bit of bandwidth overhead. Likely this will heavily limit the ability to run this on standalone VR headsets and only makes it suitable for desktop VR
> 2. screen space effects in VR have a serious problem in the result for the left eye potentially being different from the right eye. This is an issue we have in the forward+ renderer as well. The only way to solve this is to sample both left eye and right eye depth data for both  eyes, doubling the overhead in processing this.
>

These are issues for XR developers that are known and not limited to this functionality and should not be a reason for us not to move forward with this PR. Its a warning to XR developers that this is unlikely to be usable on standalone devices, and that we need to come up with a solution for the stereo issue for desktop XR (also for Forward+). Most XR developers for this reason bake their AO.

Got it.  I am reading that paper, looks like there is a decent bit of work needed to make VR SSAO work, and if even the simple case has too much overhead to be used, there's probably no point pursuing an implementation of the more complex fix.

One thing I am curious about...in my implementation the sampling radius is a function of the depth, so things farther away have a smaller radius, but they would also shift less per eye...possibly there is a magic radius setting where the AO for each eye is nearly identical since the sampled points would be farther than any depthmap discrepancies.  Possible?

author: jon1solution

body:
I did some more testing with the latest version with OpenXR on the Meta Quest 3. (I didn't do any WebXR testing this time.)

This is definitely working better than before!

- The `Parameter "env" is null.` message is no longer spammed in the editor
- The stereo effect is no longer broken, and there isn't the weird texture over the left eye
- One of the OpenGL debug messages is no longer spammed. Previously it would alternate between two different ids giving the message `texture object X to bind has a different dimensionality than texture target Y`; now it only does this for a single id. So, it seems like one case was fixed?
- Performance is somewhat better: I'm getting ~36 fps in my demo project now, rather than ~15 fps

However, it's still not perfect:

- There is still one set of OpenGL errors spammed at runtime:
  ```
  E 0:00:03:387   _gl_debug_print: GL ERROR: Source: OpenGL	Type: Error	ID: 2147483647	Severity: High	Message: number of views for the current read FBO is greater than 1
  E 0:00:03:387   _gl_debug_print: GL ERROR: Source: OpenGL	Type: Error	ID: 2147483647	Severity: High	Message: texture object 204 to bind has a different dimensionality than texture target 3553
  ```
  This leads me to believe there is still something that needs changes for multiview? Maybe something on the C++ side is still using `GL_TEXTURE_2D` rather than `GL_TEXTURE_2D_ARRAY`? But I haven't tried to find it, that's just a random guess
- With MSAA 4x enabled, the SSAO doesn't seem to be rendered. That's why I've got it turned off in all of my tests. I don't know if that's expected or not? I haven't used SSAO much, even in flat screen games

As far as the quality of the SSAO: screen-space effects (even if you manage to make them perform well) tend not to look that great in VR. Little imperfections that you would never notice in a flat screen game become very noticeable in VR, when the screen is a couple centimeters from your eyes.

That said, more options is always nice :-)

body:
> This is a small change with huge consequences that will definitely break compat. Any projects using `AudioStreamPlayer3D` instances that touched `volume_db` will definitely feel the difference.

If this is to be merged, I guess this will have to be under a project setting that's `false` by default, and could be set to `true` by default in new projects using the same approach as https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105737. The project setting would set a property in AudioServer on startup.

This way, we don't affect the behavior on existing projects, but new projects automatically get a more logical behavior.
